title: Ballard Power Systems Q4 2024 Results Summary
text: Ballard Power Systems reported Q4 2024 revenue of $24.5 million, down 48% year-over-year, with full-year 2024 revenue of $69.7 million, down 32% from 2023. Q4 gross margin improved to -13%, up 9 percentage points from the prior year. Full-year gross margin was -32%, down 11 percentage points. The company ended 2024 with $603.9 million in cash and no bank debt. Order Backlog reached $173.5 million, up 41% from Q3, while the 12-month orderbook was $98.9 million. Q4 new order intake was $75.4 million, with full-year 2024 new net order intake of approximately $113 million. Fuel cell engine shipments exceeded 660 units, representing approximately 56 megawatts, a 30% increase from 2023. Revenue segments included Heavy Duty Mobility at $16.8 million (down 42%), Stationary at $6.9 million (down 46%), and Emerging and Other Markets at $0.8 million (down 84%). The company announced a global restructuring targeting 30%+ operating expense reduction. Bus segment revenue grew 51% for the full year to $44.2 million, offsetting declines in other segments. CEO Randy MacEwen stated: 2024 was a tough year for the hydrogen and fuel cell industry. The company projects 2025 total operating expenses between $100-120 million and capital expenditures between $15-25 million, with expected back-half weighted revenue.
